tourism.intro: Finland-rooted, Nordic-deep, China-wide. From auroras to fjords, from cities to wilderness.

finland.title: Finland
finland.subtitle: Land of Auroras, Lakes, and Forests
finland.intro: Our home market. From Lapland's wilderness to the Lakeland forests, with hotel bookings, custom activities, and deep local experiences.
finland.hotelsDesc: From Lapland glass igloos to Helsinki design hotels, all price tiers covered. Long-term local partnerships secure scarce peak-season inventory at preferred rates.

hotelsItems.name: Kakslauttanen Arctic Resort
hotelsItems.desc: Watch the auroras from your bed in glass-domed igloos — among the world's top aurora hotels

hotelsItems.name: Arctic SnowHotel
hotelsItems.desc: Rebuilt every winter, with ice sauna and aurora ice suites

hotelsItems.name: Hotel St. George
hotelsItems.desc: New Nordic design, art collection, Michelin dining

hotelsItems.name: Punkaharju Hotel
hotelsItems.desc: Century-old lakeside lodge, sauna, canoe, forest

hotelsItems.name: Levi Hotel Spa
hotelsItems.desc: Slope-side ski resort with spa and family facilities

finland.activitiesDesc: Aurora viewing, ski instruction, sailing, photography sessions — every season delivers.

experiences.title: Aurora Hunting
experiences.desc: Rovaniemi/Inari professional aurora tours with real-time forecasting

experiences.title: Ski Instruction
experiences.desc: Levi/Ylläs resort ski instructors, beginner to advanced 1:1

experiences.title: Archipelago Sailing
experiences.desc: Turku archipelago/Helsinki summer sailing with skipper

experiences.title: Lakeland Retreats
experiences.desc: Cabin getaways with sauna, canoeing, foraging

experiences.title: Snowmobiling
experiences.desc: Lapland wilderness snowmobile tours with instruction and gear

finland.helsinkiDesc: Daughter of the Baltic. From the white-domed Cathedral to the rock-hewn Temppeliaukio, from the UNESCO sea fortress to the world-class Oodi library — 15 must-see Helsinki attractions.

helsinkiAttractions.name: Helsinki Cathedral
helsinkiAttractions.desc: White neoclassical cathedral atop Senate Square — the city's most iconic skyline

helsinkiAttractions.name: Suomenlinna
helsinkiAttractions.desc: Sea fortress islands, 15-min ferry from the harbor — perfect for sea-view walks and picnics

helsinkiAttractions.name: Temppeliaukio Church
helsinkiAttractions.desc: The Rock Church carved into a granite outcrop — a Nordic minimalist masterpiece with exceptional acoustics

helsinkiAttractions.name: Kauppatori Market Square
helsinkiAttractions.desc: Seaside market in the city center — must-try Finnish salmon soup (lohikeitto), Nordic snacks, souvenirs

helsinkiAttractions.name: Oodi Library
helsinkiAttractions.desc: Finland's central library, opened 2018 — world-class contemporary architecture, free entry

helsinkiAttractions.name: Design District Helsinki
helsinkiAttractions.desc: 200+ Nordic design shops, galleries, cafés, and boutiques in Helsinki's creative quarter

helsinkiAttractions.name: Löyly
helsinkiAttractions.desc: Wooden seaside complex in Hernesaari — public sauna + sea dip, the essential Finnish sauna experience

helsinkiAttractions.name: Allas Sea Pool
helsinkiAttractions.desc: Open-air seawater pools + sauna at Katajanokka in the heart of the city — unbeatable views

helsinkiAttractions.name: Esplanadi Park
helsinkiAttractions.desc: Helsinki's signature central boulevard — Kappeli's summer terrace is unmissable

helsinkiAttractions.name: Seurasaari
helsinkiAttractions.desc: Open-air museum island with traditional Finnish wooden buildings relocated from around the country

helsinkiAttractions.name: Uspenski Cathedral
helsinkiAttractions.desc: Northern Europe's largest Orthodox cathedral — red-brick Russian architecture overlooking the harbor

helsinkiAttractions.name: Sibelius Monument
helsinkiAttractions.desc: Memorial to Finland's national composer Jean Sibelius — 600 steel organ pipes

helsinkiAttractions.name: Kiasma
helsinkiAttractions.desc: Flagship Nordic contemporary art museum — the building itself is a work of art

helsinkiAttractions.name: Ateneum
helsinkiAttractions.desc: Finland's national gallery — Golden Age art including Gallen-Kallela and other masters

helsinkiAttractions.name: Old Market Hall
helsinkiAttractions.desc: Indoor market open since 1889 — traditional Finnish food, coffee, pastries, seafood, deli

finland.dayTripsDesc: Four Nordic excursions from Helsinki ranging from half-day to three days — covering Finland's gems plus cross-sea neighboring capitals.

dayTripsItems.name: Porvoo
dayTripsItems.desc: Finland's most photogenic old town — red wooden houses, riverside market, cafés and craft shops

dayTripsItems.name: Fiskars Village
dayTripsItems.desc: Nordic design pilgrimage — historic ironworks transformed into an artist village with workshops, galleries, restaurants

dayTripsItems.name: Tallinn
dayTripsItems.desc: UNESCO medieval old town — cobblestone streets, city walls, church spires, excellent value

dayTripsItems.name: Stockholm
dayTripsItems.desc: Nordic capital, fourteen-island city — royal palace, Vasa Museum, archipelago cruises

finland.diningDesc: Curated Helsinki restaurants — from Michelin fine dining and new-Nordic creativity, to Chinese home-style and convenient near-hotel options.

diningHotelItems.name: Ravintola Konnikiwa
diningHotelItems.desc: Inside the Aalto Metro station mall, supermarket directly across. Buffet-style — sushi, Chinese dishes, fruit, dessert, coffee and tea. Convenient with broad selection.

diningHotelItems.name: Ravintola Minmax
diningHotelItems.desc: About 700 m from the venue. Table service and buffet — steamed buns, dumplings, noodle soup, mantou, leaning Sichuan. Simple interior but authentic flavors.

diningHotelItems.name: Ravintola Sway
diningHotelItems.desc: Lovely restaurant ambience, both buffet and table service. The only downside is the longer walk.

diningWesternItems.name: Ravintola Palace
diningWesternItems.desc: One of Helsinki's top Michelin restaurants — a rare Finnish two-star. Stunning sea views, classic Nordic high-end fine dining. Strong seafood and Nordic flavors, very formal service, excellent wine pairing.
diningWesternItems.best: Business dining · anniversaries · first Michelin experience

diningWesternItems.name: Restaurant Olo
diningWesternItems.desc: Classic new-Nordic cuisine — many local foodies call it the 'most Finnish' Michelin. Complete tasting menu, Nordic forest-driven style, venison, mushrooms, and fermented ingredients shine.
diningWesternItems.best: To experience the essence of Nordic Cuisine

diningWesternItems.name: Ravintola Nokka
diningWesternItems.desc: Many Asian guests rate this very highly — premium ingredients without being too 'wild'. Excellent handling of Nordic game (venison, king crab, fish), comfortable atmosphere without stiffness.

diningWesternItems.name: Restaurant Demo
diningWesternItems.desc: Long-established Helsinki Michelin, very consistent. Balanced flavors, mature service, never as cold as some Nordic restaurants.

diningWesternItems.name: Goodwin The Steak House
diningWesternItems.desc: If you just want a solid steak, this is a safe bet. Reliable steak quality, seaside location, mature service.

diningWesternItems.name: Restaurant Zetor
diningWesternItems.desc: The fun take on Finnish dining — tractor-themed (yes really), lively atmosphere, traditional Finnish dishes well executed, great for visiting foreigners. Much cheaper than fine dining at €25–50 for a great meal.

diningWesternItems.name: Kappeli Ravintola
diningWesternItems.desc: Operating since 1867, the 'pearl of Esplanadi'. Large glass conservatory facing Esplanadi Park, 19th-century European café atmosphere. Try the salmon soup, reindeer, Nordic fish, afternoon coffee.

diningWesternItems.name: Ravintola Ragu
diningWesternItems.desc: One of the Helsinki restaurants 'closest to Michelin level but more relaxed'. Finnish and Nordic seasonal ingredients combined with Italian cooking logic and wine culture — modern European fine dining, quiet and refined.

diningChineseItems.name: Jinguanting Brokadi
diningChineseItems.desc: One of Helsinki's most established Chinese restaurants among the Finnish-Chinese community and international visitors. Located inside Tripla mall above Pasila railway station — the largest commercial complex in the Nordics, with seamless connections to central Helsinki and the airport. Modern commercial interior fused with Eastern elements, suitable for business meetings, cultural exchanges, group dining.

diningChineseItems.name: Ravintola Liu
diningChineseItems.desc: Located in Vantaa adjacent to Helsinki-Vantaa International Airport, a key transit hub in the northern capital region. Traditional Northeastern Chinese flavors — robust northern Chinese food culture preserving the rich, savory, fire-controlled traditions of Dongbei cuisine.

diningChineseItems.name: Happy Food Garden
diningChineseItems.desc: Popular among local Chinese and international diners. Specializes in Sichuan hotpot, Chinese BBQ, traditional Sichuan dishes. Lively ambience for friend gatherings, business meals, team activities. Authentic Chinese flavors, rich menu, private rooms available.

diningChineseItems.name: Dongbei House
diningChineseItems.desc: Known locally as 'Dongbeihu' (Northeastern Tiger). Famous for authentic Northeastern Chinese and classic home-style dishes. Clean, generous interior, easy access, great for business and friends. Specialties: pork in vinegar sauce, cumin lamb, sautéed eggplant/potato/peppers. Private rooms available.

diningChineseItems.name: Leaf Restaurant
diningChineseItems.location: Forum Mall ground floor · Near Central Station
diningChineseItems.desc: In the basement of Forum Mall, minutes from Helsinki Central Station. Looks like a sushi buffet, but those in the know order from the 'hidden Chinese menu' — Sichuan dishes have a strong reputation. Boiled, fish-flavored, dry-pot, Sichuan, fried noodles closer to mainland flavors — popular with Chinese students and Asian travelers seeking real Chinese taste.

norway.title: Norway
norway.subtitle: Fjords and the Aurora Coast
norway.intro: From Tromsø to Lofoten, Geirangerfjord to the Atlantic Road — Norway's most breathtaking northern landscapes.

routesItems.name: Lofoten Islands
routesItems.desc: Red fishing villages, snow peaks meeting the sea

routesItems.name: Tromsø
routesItems.desc: Gateway to the Arctic, husky sledding & auroras

routesItems.name: Atlantic Road
routesItems.desc: One of the world's most scenic drives

routesItems.name: Molde
routesItems.desc: City of Roses, Hundred Hills viewpoint

routesItems.name: Longyearbyen
routesItems.desc: Svalbard — the edge of the Arctic wilderness

activitiesItems.name: King Crab Safari
activitiesItems.desc: Northern Norway king crab fishing with on-site tasting

iceland.title: Iceland
iceland.subtitle: Land of Fire and Ice
iceland.intro: Waterfalls, glaciers, geysers, black-sand beaches — every inch of Iceland is a geological marvel.

routesItems.name: Golden Circle
routesItems.desc: Þingvellir National Park + Geysir + Gullfoss waterfall

routesItems.name: Snæfellsnes Peninsula
routesItems.desc: Iceland in miniature — volcanoes, waterfalls, black church

denmark.title: Denmark
denmark.subtitle: Hidden Islands of the North Atlantic
denmark.intro: Beyond Copenhagen, Denmark's most rewarding destinations are its remote territories — Greenland and the Faroe Islands.

routesItems.name: Greenland
routesItems.desc: Ilulissat icefjord, Nuuk Arctic culture, midnight sun

routesItems.name: Faroe Islands
routesItems.desc: Cliffs, waterfalls, grass-roof villages, North Atlantic isolation

routesItems.name: Copenhagen
routesItems.desc: Fairytale city, birthplace of Nordic design and New Nordic cuisine

china.title: China
china.subtitle: Jiangnan — Eight Curated Three-Day Tours
china.intro: Our China itineraries cover the Jiangnan classics — southern Anhui's Huizhou heritage villages, Suzhou's classical gardens, Hangzhou's West Lake, and Shanghai's metropolitan elegance — across eight carefully crafted three-day programs with transport, 4-star accommodation, meals, attraction tickets, English-speaking guide, and travel insurance.
china.anhuiToursSubtitle: Three-Day Tours · Huangshan · Huizhou · Qiyun · Anhui · Suzhou · Hangzhou · Suzhou-Hangzhou · Shanghai
china.anhuiToursIntro: Eight three-day itineraries spanning the Huizhou heritage of southern Anhui to the gardens and water-towns of Jiangnan and the cosmopolitan elegance of Shanghai. Anhui routes depart from Hefei (38-seat coach, RMB 50/meal, 4-star hotel); Jiangnan/Shanghai routes include airport pickup (37-seat coach, RMB 60/meal, 4-diamond hotel). All tours include English-speaking guide, attraction tickets, and travel personal accident insurance.

anhuiTours.title: Three-Day Tour of Huangshan
anhuiTours.tagline: After Huangshan, no other mountain is worth seeing
anhuiTours.summary: Huangshan Scenic Area + Chengkan ancient village + Tunxi Old Street + Xixinan Wetland — combining hiking and cable car for the iconic pines and sea of clouds.

items.name: Depart Hefei to Huangshan by coach
Chengkan ancient village
items.desc: Praised by Zhu Xi as 'the number one village in Jiangnan' — millennium-old Huizhou village with exquisite carvings, evening fish-lantern performance

items.name: National AAAAA Huangshan Scenic Area
items.desc: Coach to Yungu Temple, cable car or hike to Bai'e Ridge, Shixin Peak, Bright Summit, Yuping Tower — view the famous Huangshan pines

items.name: Tunxi Old Street by night
items.desc: Built in the Song Dynasty, best-preserved Song-Ming-Qing pedestrian street in China

items.name: Xixinan Wetland
items.desc: Surrounded by maple-poplar forest, streams and stone bridges — pastoral poetry in morning mist

anhuiTours.title: Three-Day Tour of Huizhou
anhuiTours.tagline: White walls, dark tiles, water under bridges
anhuiTours.summary: Tangyue Archways + Huizhou Ancient City + Xin'an River Gallery + Xixinan + Tunxi Old Street + Hongcun — Huizhou cultural deep-dive.

items.name: Tangyue Archway Group
items.desc: 'The Number One Village in Jiangnan' — seven ancient archways, ancestral halls, live filial-piety performances

items.name: Huizhou Ancient City
items.desc: Birthplace of Huizhou learning, three-carving art, ancient government office, evening fish-lantern parade

days.title: Xin'an River — Xixinan — Tunxi Old Street

items.name: Xin'an River Scenic Gallery boat tour
items.desc: 100-mile picturesque corridor, ancient Huizhou merchant route, riverside villages, millennial camphor trees

items.name: Xixinan Wetland
items.desc: Real-life Wizard of Oz — maple forest, streams, stone bridges, Jiangnan charm

items.name: Tunxi Old Street
items.desc: Best-preserved pedestrian commercial street in China, Song-Ming-Qing architecture

items.name: Hongcun ancient village
items.desc: 'The village in Chinese paintings' — unique ox-shaped water system, exquisite carvings at Chengzhi Hall — known as the 'Folk Palace Museum'

anhuiTours.title: Three-Day Trip to Qiyun Mountain
anhuiTours.tagline: Danxia landform · Taoist culture · No.1 mountain in Jiangnan
anhuiTours.summary: Huizhou Grand Canyon + Qiyun Mountain + Yuehua Street + Chengkan + Qiankou Residential Houses — landscape meets Taoism.

items.name: Huizhou Grand Canyon
items.desc: Cliff waterfalls, turquoise pools, ancient pines — optional thrilling rafting

items.name: Qiyun Mountain
items.desc: Danxia landform, Taoist heritage, 'No.1 mountain in Jiangnan', countless historic stone carvings

items.name: Bamboo rafting at Qiyun foothill
items.desc: Banks lined with millennial trees, graceful river scenery

items.name: Chengkan
items.desc: Naturally formed Bagua-pattern village, two national-treasure ancient buildings, Hui-style carvings

items.name: Qiankou Residential Houses
items.desc: 'For palaces go to Beijing; for residential houses come to Qiankou' — Ming-dynasty Hui-style ensemble

anhuiTours.title: Three-Day Tour of Southern Anhui & Jingchuan
anhuiTours.tagline: Li Bai's Peach Blossom Pond · Chinese Xuan paper culture
anhuiTours.summary: Peach Blossom Pond + Chaji ancient village + Moon Bay + Pingyuan secret realm + China Xuan Paper Culture Park.

days.title: Hefei — Jing County — Pingyuan — Moon Bay

items.name: Pingyuan secret realm
items.desc: 'Mini Li River of southern Anhui' — bamboo raft amongst rocks, Fengyang market team-building

items.name: Moon Bay Scenic Area
items.desc: 100-mile bamboo forest, classic film & TV location

items.name: Peach Blossom Pond (Taohuatan)
items.desc: Made famous by Li Bai's poem 'Farewell to Wang Lun' — emerald ripples, ancient riverbank trees

items.name: Chaji ancient village
items.desc: Small bridges and flowing water, Ming-Qing carved-beam buildings, ancient ancestral halls

items.name: Chinese Xuan Paper Culture Park
items.desc: National intangible heritage — handmade Xuan paper process, optional papermaking & rubbing experience

anhuiTours.title: Three-Day Tour of Suzhou
anhuiTours.tagline: Heaven on earth, gardens above all
anhuiTours.summary: Tiger Hill + Humble Administrator's Garden + Hanshan Temple + Lingering Garden + Zhouzhuang — two of China's four famous gardens plus the No.1 water town.

items.name: Tiger Hill
items.desc: 'The top scenic spot in Wu' — centered around the thousand-year leaning Yunyan Pagoda and Spring-Autumn Sword Pool, iconic 2500-year Wu culture site

days.title: Humble Administrator's Garden — Hanshan Temple — Lingering Garden

items.name: Humble Administrator's Garden (Zhuozheng Yuan)
items.desc: Suzhou's largest surviving classical garden, World Heritage site, water-soul, changing view at every step — pinnacle of Jiangnan gardens

items.name: Hanshan Temple
items.desc: Made world-famous by Zhang Ji's poem 'A Night Mooring by Maple Bridge', a millennium-old Suzhou temple, classic 'Maple Bridge at Night' landmark

items.name: Lingering Garden (Liu Yuan)
items.desc: One of China's four famous gardens, World Heritage site, renowned for architectural spatial art, 700-m winding corridors and Guanyun Peak's rare stones — recreating worlds within inches

items.name: Zhouzhuang
items.desc: 'China's No.1 Water Town', 900-year history, well-shaped waterways, 14 ancient bridges, Ming-Qing residences. Shuangqiao, Shenting, Zhangting are landmarks — quintessential Jiangnan small bridges & flowing water

items.name: Lunch, airport transfer, end of tour

anhuiTours.title: Three-Day Tour of Hangzhou
anhuiTours.tagline: West Lake serenity · Lingyin Temple Zen
anhuiTours.summary: Lingyin Temple + West Lake + Xixi Wetland + Songcheng + Liangzhu Cultural Heritage Park — World Heritage sites plus 5,000-year civilization site.

days.title: Airport pickup — Lingyin Temple — West Lake

items.name: Lingyin Temple
items.desc: Also known as 'Yunlin Temple', at the foot of Lingyin Mountain west of West Lake — serene and quiet, the earliest famous temple in Hangzhou, preserving rare relics from many dynasties

items.name: West Lake Scenic Area
items.desc: Witnessed the development of Chinese Buddhist, Taoist culture and tea-Zen traditions. Inscribed on the World Heritage List on June 24, 2011

items.name: Xixi National Wetland Park
items.desc: China's first national wetland park integrating urban, agricultural and cultural wetlands. 5A-level attraction, picturesque 'a stream of water, a wisp of mist'

items.name: Songcheng
items.desc: Crossing past and present, twenty theaters and hundreds of performances — one day is not enough to see everything

items.name: Liangzhu Cultural Heritage Park
items.desc: Dating back ~5,300-4,300 years, an important representative of early regional civilization in China, the 'Holy Land that provides evidence for 5,000 years of Chinese civilization'

items.name: Lunch, airport transfer, end of tour

anhuiTours.title: Three-Day Tour of Suzhou & Hangzhou
anhuiTours.tagline: Jiangnan double-city — gardens meet West Lake
anhuiTours.summary: Humble Administrator's Garden + Hanshan Temple + Wuzhen + Songcheng + West Lake — best of Suzhou and Hangzhou in one trip.

days.title: Airport pickup — Humble Administrator's Garden — Hanshan Temple

items.name: Humble Administrator's Garden
items.desc: Suzhou's largest classical garden, World Heritage site, water-focused with a new view at every step — pinnacle of Jiangnan gardens

items.name: Hanshan Temple
items.desc: Famous worldwide thanks to Zhang Ji's 'A Night Mooring by Maple Bridge' — a thousand-year-old Suzhou temple, classic 'Night Mooring' landmark

items.name: Wuzhen
items.desc: 5A-level ancient town in Tongxiang, Zhejiang — millennium-old Jiangnan water-town, 'the last waterside residence in China', permanent venue of the World Internet Conference

items.name: Songcheng
items.desc: Spans past and present — twenty theaters, hundreds of performances, never enough time for everything

items.name: West Lake Scenic Area
items.desc: Witnessed Chinese Buddhist, Taoist, tea-Zen traditions — World Heritage site

items.name: Lunch, airport transfer, end of tour

anhuiTours.title: Three-Day Shanghai Itinerary
anhuiTours.tagline: Magic City nights — history meets modernity
anhuiTours.summary: Yu Garden + City God Temple + Sinan Road + Nanjing Road + Shanghai Museum + Xintiandi + Tianzifang + Oriental Pearl + the Bund + Postal Museum — Shanghai's classics in 3 days.

days.title: Airport pickup — Yu Garden — City God Temple — Sinan Rd — Nanjing Rd

items.name: Yu Garden
items.desc: Originally a Ming-Dynasty private garden, ranks first among Shanghai's five existing classical gardens

items.name: City God Temple
items.desc: Built in the Ming Dynasty, a Taoist temple of the Orthodox Unity Sect — together with City God snacks, Lotus Pond, Mid-Lake Pavilion and Nine-Turn Bridge forms Shanghai's iconic Old Town

items.name: Sinan Road
items.desc: Stroll or cycle along plane-tree avenues and admire French-style villas lining both sides

items.name: Nanjing Road Pedestrian Street
items.desc: Asymmetrical layout with a 4.2-meter 'Golden Belt' through the entire street, 34 differently-designed flower beds

days.title: Shanghai Museum — Xintiandi — Tianzifang — Oriental Pearl — Huangpu ferry

items.name: Shanghai Museum (People's Square Branch)
items.desc: Rich collections of bronzes, ceramics, calligraphy and paintings, jade — deep dive into Chinese history

items.name: Xintiandi
items.desc: Boutique shops, treasure hunting, relaxing café moments

items.name: Tianzifang
items.desc: Rebuilt from Shanghai's unique Shikumen architecture — creative base for artists, maze-like alleyways, retro handmade craft shops

items.name: Oriental Pearl Tower (exterior)
items.desc: Standing in Lujiazui, Pudong, facing the Bund across the Huangpu River — 468 m, eleven spheres inspired by 'big and small pearls falling on a jade plate'

items.name: Huangpu River ferry (Jinling East Rd — Dongchang Rd)
items.desc: Admire the exotic Bund architecture, gaze across to the Lujiazui skyscrapers — history and modernity in perfect blend

items.name: Shanghai Postal Museum
items.desc: Located in the Shanghai General Post Office Building — train-carriage post offices, vintage mailboxes, the history of Chinese postal service

items.name: The Bund
items.desc: Along the Huangpu River for ~1.5 km — Shanghai's symbolic landmark, famous for its international architecture and the stunning night view

items.name: Lunch, airport transfer, end of tour

anhuiServiceItems: Accommodation: 2 nights at 4-star hotel or 4-diamond rated boutique (twin room, breakfast)
Meals: 5 full meals (RMB 50 Anhui / RMB 60 Jiangnan & Shanghai per person per meal)
Guide: 3 days of service by one English-speaking guide
Insurance: Travel personal accident insurance, RMB 200,000 / person
Extras: 2 bottles of mineral water per person per day
china.regionComingSoon: Itineraries for this region are in the works — coming soon. Contact us for a custom program.

guide.title: China Travel Guide
guide.subtitle: First Time in China — A Practical Guide for International Visitors
guide.intro: Everything you need to sort out before landing in China: entry rules, flights, transfers, staying connected, and how to pay. On our tours we handle everything on the ground — this is what to prepare before you fly.

items.title: Visa & Entry
items.body: Finland and most European passports currently enjoy 30-day visa-free entry to China (policy in force through the end of 2026). Travelers from other countries can often use the 240-hour visa-free transit when continuing to a third country, or apply for a standard L tourist visa. On arrival you complete an arrival card and fingerprint scan — keep your hotel address and onward ticket handy.

items.title: Flights & Getting There
items.body: Direct flights connect Helsinki with Shanghai in about 9–10 hours. For our Jiangnan tours, fly into Shanghai Pudong (PVG): Suzhou and Hangzhou are under two hours away by high-speed rail, Hefei about three. We can also arrange connecting domestic flights for itineraries further inland.

items.title: Transfers & Getting Around
items.body: Our packages include airport pickup and a private coach throughout the tour. For independent days: high-speed rail tickets are bought with your passport (12306 app or station counters), DiDi — China's Uber — works in English, and metro systems accept Alipay QR codes. Always carry your passport; it is required for trains and hotel check-in.

items.title: SIM Cards & Internet
items.body: Easiest option: an international eSIM (e.g. Airalo, Holafly) activated before landing — roaming data reaches Google, WhatsApp, and Instagram normally. Local SIM cards from China Unicom or China Mobile at the airport are cheaper but run on the local network, where most Western apps and sites are blocked. Hotel Wi-Fi is everywhere but subject to the same restrictions.

items.title: Payments
items.body: China is nearly cashless — Alipay and WeChat Pay cover almost every shop, taxi, and street stall. Both accept foreign Visa and Mastercard: download the app, verify with your passport, link your card, and pay by scanning QR codes. Foreign bank cards are accepted at major hotels only, so keep a small amount of cash (RMB 200–500) as a fallback. Tipping is not expected anywhere.

items.title: Useful Apps
items.body: Set these up before departure: Alipay (payments, transport, translation mini-apps), WeChat (messaging — universal in China), DiDi (ride-hailing), Amap or Apple Maps (Google Maps is unreliable in China), and a translator app. Our guides speak English throughout the tour, so apps are mainly for your free time.

cities.name: Beijing
cities.desc: Imperial capital — Forbidden City, Great Wall, hutongs

cities.name: Shanghai
cities.desc: The Bund, contemporary art, Michelin dining

cities.name: Xi'an
cities.desc: Silk Road origin — Terracotta Army, city walls

cities.name: Chengdu
cities.desc: Land of Plenty — pandas, Sichuan cuisine, slow life

cities.name: Hangzhou
cities.desc: West Lake, Longjing tea, Song-dynasty heritage

cities.name: Lijiang
cities.desc: Ancient town, Jade Dragon Snow Mountain

cities.name: Xiamen
cities.desc: Garden city by the sea, Gulangyu, Min culture

education.schoolsSubtitle: Institutions — Top Finnish & European Education Providers
education.schoolsIntro: Universities, polytechnics, high schools, and specialist institutes such as science centres and sports academies. Detailed institution profiles, admission requirements, and program information.

schools.name: Heureka Science Centre
schools.desc: Finland's interactive science centre for hands-on STEAM learning
schools.intro: Heureka, the Finnish Science Centre in Vantaa, has made science a hands-on experience since 1989. Its interactive exhibitions, digital planetarium, and learning labs invite visitors of every age to experiment for themselves, making it one of Finland's most visited attractions and a flagship destination for STEAM education. For visiting school groups we arrange guided tours and workshop programs aligned with the Finnish curriculum.
facts: 20 minutes from central Helsinki by train

schools.name: Kisakallio Sports Institute
schools.desc: Sports institute for training camps, coaching, and physical education
schools.intro: Founded in 1949 on a lakeside campus in Lohja, Kisakallio is one of Finland's leading sports institutes. It combines Olympic-standard training facilities with on-campus accommodation and dining, hosting international training camps, coaching education, and physical education programs for visiting groups of all levels — just an hour from Helsinki.
facts: International camps & PE programs for groups

schools.name: Kuortane Olympic Training Center
schools.desc: Olympic-level training environment and sports education
schools.intro: Kuortane Olympic Training Center in western Finland is one of the country's official Olympic training centers. Elite athletes in athletics, wrestling, and volleyball train alongside its sports academy and sports high school, and the center runs year-round camps that combine training, performance testing, and coaching education — open to international groups as well.
facts: Athletics hall, ice rinks & testing laboratory
Hosts international elite and youth camps

schools.name: University of Helsinki
schools.intro: Founded in 1640, the University of Helsinki is Finland's oldest and largest university and a fixture of the QS world top 100. Its eleven faculties span sciences, humanities, law, and medicine, with the neoclassical main building standing on Senate Square in the heart of the capital. Bachelor's and master's programs are offered in English across a wide range of fields.

schools.name: Aalto University
schools.intro: Aalto University was created in 2010 by merging Finland's leading universities of technology, business, and art & design. Its Otaniemi campus in Espoo — including the iconic Dipoli building — is the heart of Finland's startup and innovation scene. Aalto is known for combining engineering with design thinking and for one of Europe's most active entrepreneurship communities.

schools.name: Haaga-Helia UAS
schools.intro: Haaga-Helia University of Applied Sciences in Helsinki specialises in business, hospitality, tourism, and communication. Education is practice-oriented and built around close industry partnerships, with the main campus in Pasila a few minutes from central Helsinki. Its hotel and restaurant management programs are among the best known in the Nordics.

schools.name: LAB UAS
schools.intro: LAB University of Applied Sciences operates campuses in Lahti and Lappeenranta, with strengths in design, circular economy, health care, and business. Formed in 2020, it works tightly with regional industry and is recognised for its Institute of Design — one of Finland's foremost design schools.

schools.name: University of Jyväskylä
schools.intro: The University of Jyväskylä is Finland's leading centre of educational research and teacher training — the heart of the famous Finnish education model. Its Seminaarinmäki campus features buildings by Alvar Aalto, and the university is also nationally unique in sport and health sciences, making it a natural partner for education and sports study programs alike.
facts: Only sport sciences faculty in Finland
Central Finland · lakeside city of Jyväskylä

schools.name: University of Turku
schools.intro: The University of Turku, founded in 1920 in Finland's oldest city, is a comprehensive research university with particular strength in marine and life sciences, medicine, and future technologies. Its riverside campus sits beside Turku Cathedral, and the university is a key partner in the region's biotech and maritime clusters.
facts: Strong in marine & life sciences, medicine

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: About Finuo — A Travel Agency in Finland Bridging China and Northern Europe
about.intro: Finuo is a Helsinki-based travel agency in Finland, connecting China and Europe across Nordic travel, study-abroad consulting, and MICE event services. With 10+ years of Nordic depth and a trilingual team (Chinese / English / Finnish), we aim to be your most reliable Finland-based partner — whether you're planning an aurora trip, a custom itinerary across Norway, Iceland, and Denmark, applying to a Finnish university, or organizing a conference in Helsinki.
about.teamDesc: Local Chinese expats and Nordic specialists, fluent in cultural nuance and commercial logic.
about.partnersDesc: Long-term partnerships with Finnish universities, local governments, industry associations, and top resorts.
